item.title: Bulgaria Lawmakers Approve Radev’s Cabinet After Landslide Win
item.description: Bulgaria’s parliament voted in the nation’s first single-party government with a majority this century after Rumen Radev’s landslide victory, ushering in another European Union leader critical of the bloc’s mainstream.

item.title: South East Water CEO Quits After Pressure From UK Lawmakers
item.description: The embattled chief executive officer of the UK’s South East Water resigned after a campaign by lawmakers and customers in the wake of multiple outages across Kent that affected tens of thousands of people.

item.title: Mariana Mazzucato Thinks We Need More Moonshots
item.description: A conversation about the political economy of innovation.

item.title: Odd Lots: Mariana Mazzucato on the Need for Moonshots (Podcast)
item.description: Today’s guest Mariana Mazzucato is one of our most requested. Mazzucato, who is the director of the University College London Institute for Innovation and Public Purpose, specializes in the political economy of technological development and public sector investment. In our conversation, recorded in Madrid while at the Bloomberg CityLab conference, she explains her concept of the “mission economy,” her definition of state capacity, how to prevent top talent from fleeing to the private sector, and

item.title: Food Prices Rise to Highest in Three Years on Iran War Costs
item.description: Global food prices rose to their highest in more than three years as the Iran war disrupted supply chains, raising the prospect of higher bills for shoppers.

item.title: TSMC and Sony to Form Joint Venture on Image Sensors
item.description: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. and Sony Semiconductor Solutions Corporation are planning to form a joint venture to build next-generation image sensors.

item.title: Iran Turns to China Rail Link to Try to Bypass US Blockade
item.description: Iran is ramping up trade with China via rail in a bid to blunt the impact of a US blockade of its ports and adapt to pressure designed to strangle its economy.

item.title: Granite Asia Taps DBS’ Wealthy Clients for Private Credit Fund
item.description: Singapore-based firm Granite Asia has engaged the private banking arm of DBS Group Holding Ltd. to raise additional capital for its first private credit vehicle, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Intertek Investors Push Firm to Engage After $12 Billion EQT Bid
item.description: Some top investors in Intertek Group Plc are pushing the British product-testing company to engage with private equity suitor EQT AB, people with knowledge of the matter said.

item.title: SoftBank Cuts Target for OpenAI Margin Loan by 40% to $6 Billion
item.description: SoftBank Group Corp. has downsized plans for a $10 billion margin loan backed by its OpenAI stake after facing hesitation from some creditors, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Lagarde Sees Euro-Stablecoin Risks for Banking, Monetary Policy
item.description: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde said that even euro-denominated stablecoins would create risks to financial stability and monetary-policy transmission, questioning the case for introducing such instruments.

item.title: Airtel Delays IPO of Mobile Money Unit to Second Half of 2026
item.description: Airtel Africa Plc said it plans an initial public offering of its mobile money business in the second half of this year, delaying the sale by a few months.

item.title: Nintendo Plans Switch 2 Price Increase as Outlook Disappoints
item.description: Nintendo Co. said it will increase the price of its Switch 2 console to $500 from $450, acknowledging pressure on profitability of its flagship device heading into its second year on the market.

item.title: US Said to Suspect Nvidia Chips Smuggled to Alibaba Via Thailand
item.description: A key company behind Thailand’s national AI effort is suspected of helping to smuggle billions of dollars worth of Super Micro Computer Inc. servers containing advanced Nvidia Corp. chips to China, with Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. one of multiple end customers, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: German Industrial Production Unexpectedly Falls for Second Month
item.description: German industrial production unexpectedly fell in March for a second month, adding to worries for Europe’s largest economy as it grapples with the Iran war.

item.title: Air India to Cut Costs, Reduce Flights as War Worsens Financial Struggles
item.description: Air India Ltd. is looking to implement measures to cut costs and reduce flights in the wake of the Iran war, people familiar with the matter said, just as the unprofitable airline searches for a new leader to navigate an exceptionally difficult period.

item.title: UK’s Intertek Spurns EQT’s Latest Bid Worth £8.9 Billion
item.description: Intertek Group Plc rejected the latest takeover offer from EQT AB as the product testing business continues to resist the private equity firm’s advances.

item.title: British Airways Owner Sees Profit Hit as War Hikes Fuel Bill
item.description: British Airways owner IAG SA said profit and free cash flow for the year will be lower than previously anticipated amid a surging fuel bill, driven by soaring oil prices from the Middle East war.

item.title: Xero Founder Returns New Zealand Prize After Misconduct Claims
item.description: Rod Drury, the founder of ASX-listed accounting software firm Xero, has handed back the New Zealander of the Year prize he won just weeks ago following allegations of sexual misconduct involving former employees.

item.title: Stellantis to Deepen China Pact with Joint EV, Spain Plant Shift
item.description: Stellantis NV plans to extend its cooperation with China’s Leapmotor, including co-developing an electric model under the Opel brand, shifting ownership of a plant in Spain and expanding joint purchasing.

item.title: BlackRock Says Emerging Market Bonds Are Headed for Strong Year
item.description: BlackRock Inc.’s chief of emerging markets debt is counting on another strong year for bond returns as the weaker dollar improves financing conditions.

item.title: Rich Families Are Target Clients for New AI-Powered Consultancy
item.description: When Tony Cowell retired in 2023 from a three-decade career advising private equity and hedge funds from the Cayman Islands at KPMG LLP, AI was still relatively nascent.

item.title: US, Iran Clash Near Hormuz as Response on Proposed Deal Awaited
item.description: The US and Iran clashed near the Strait of Hormuz, an escalation that threatens to further fracture a fragile ceasefire as the two sides discuss a permanent end to the war.

item.title: TSMC Sales Grow 17.5% on Extended AI Buildout Boom
item.description: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. reported a 17.5% increase in its sales, highlighting sustained spending by hyperscalers bankrolling the AI boom.

item.title: Pounds Edges Higher as Labour Suffers Early Losses
Myanmar Says Giant 11,000-Carat Ruby Found in Mandalay Region
item.description: Myanmar has announced the discovery of a massive ruby weighing 11,000 carats (2.2 kilograms), a rare find even for the country’s central gemstone hub of Mogok near Mandalay.

item.title: Pakistan Shuns Spot LNG as It Bets on Potential Hormuz Easing
item.description: Pakistan opted not to buy urgent cargoes of liquefied natural gas on the spot market, betting that hostilities which have closed the Strait of Hormuz will ease and cheaper supplies from Qatar will arrive soon.

item.title: Macquarie CFO Says Private Credit Angst Due to Liquidity Issues
item.description: Worries in the private credit market stem from retail-driven liquidity problems rather than the health of the underlining portfolio, according to Frank Kwok, Chief Financial Officer at Macquarie Group Ltd.

item.title: New Zealand Government Repeats Call for Kiwibank to Explore Share Sale
item.description: New Zealand’s government is reiterating it wants state-owned Kiwibank to become a stronger competitor and that it should be open to raising capital to fund growth, including via a partial share sale.

item.title: Baidu’s AI Chip Unit Plans Dual IPO in Shanghai and Hong Kong
item.description: Baidu Inc.’s chip unit Kunlunxin is planning an initial public offering on Shanghai’s Nasdaq-style bourse in addition to a separate listing plan in Hong Kong as the Chinese search engine giant looks to taps investor appetite for semiconductor stocks.
